title: patchChanelog remove additional text from changelog
author: egorikftp
body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
I sometimes add special highlights related to change. 
For example:

```
 # Gradle plugin changelog

## Unreleased

## 0.4.0 - 2026-02-25

### Added

- Some release notes

Additional note for customers.

Structured info:

1. **L1** - text
2. **L2** - text
3. **L3** - text

## 0.3.0 - 2026-02-24

### Changed

- Introduce breaking changes

| Before | After       |
|--------|-------------|
| `test` | `test test` |
      
```

After running `patchChangelog` it remove everything that not related to predefined sections.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Keep additional text without changes

title: Simple way to specify the version prefix for each version by name
author: jodiew
body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
Some of the comparison links in the `CHANGELOG.md` file in this repository (and others) don't work because the plugin has a default tag prefix of "v", but not all the tags have a "v" prefix.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
A property in the changelog section of `build.gradle.kts` that lets you specify a function that takes the version name and returns the prefix to use, similar to the `sectionUrlBuilder`. Something like
```kts
changelog {
    groups.empty()
    repositoryUrl = providers.gradleProperty("pluginRepositoryUrl")
    versionPrefixBuilder = VersionPrefixBuilder { version -> when (version) {
        "1.0.0" -> "v"
        else -> ""
    }   }
}
```

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
- it works to use `sectionUrlBuilder`, by re-creating the default behaviour and just adding new values for the prefixes, this would just be a bit more clear
- you could re-release with renamed tags, but that seems like bad practice
- `versionPrefix = ""` to set a different default prefix doesn't work as the tags don't all have the same prefix

**Additional context**
I made a related PR in https://github.com/JetBrains/intellij-platform-plugin-template/pull/557 that fixes the `CHANGELOG.md` in that repository with the `sectionUrlBuilder`. I would also be interested in doing the PR for this if it's decided to be helpful and appropriate.

title: patchChangelog command significantly update changelog and break existing data
author: egorikftp
body:
**Describe the bug**
I have changelog in following format

```md
# Plugin Changelog

## [Unreleased]

### Fixed

- Unreleased fix

## [0.17.2](https://github.com/ComposeGears/Valkyrie/releases/tag/0.17.2) - 2025-10-11

### Fixed

- Fix PSI parsing

```

Then bump version to `0.17.3` and run `./gradlew patchChangelog` command. The changelog changes in this format

```md
# Plugin Changelog

## [Unreleased]

## [0.17.3] - 2025-10-14

### Fixed

- Unreleased fix

## [0.17.2](https://github.com/ComposeGears/Valkyrie/releases/tag/[0.17.2]) - 2025-10-11

### Fixed

- Fix PSI parsing

[Unreleased]: https://github.com/ComposeGears/Valkyrie/compare/0.17.3...HEAD
[0.17.3]: https://github.com/ComposeGears/Valkyrie/compare/0.17.2...0.17.3
[0.17.2]: https://github.com/ComposeGears/Valkyrie/commits/0.17.2
```

1. broken link in header
2. Added `[Unreleased]`, `[0.17.3]`,  `[0.17.2]` links block at the end of file


**Expected behavior**
```md
# Plugin Changelog

## [Unreleased]

## [0.17.3](https://github.com/ComposeGears/Valkyrie/releases/tag/0.17.3) - 2025-10-14

### Fixed

- Unreleased fix

## [0.17.2](https://github.com/ComposeGears/Valkyrie/releases/tag/0.17.2) - 2025-10-11

### Fixed

- Fix PSI parsing
```


**Environment:**
 - OS: macOS
 - Gradle Changelog Plugin Version: 2.4.0
 - Gradle Version: 9.1.0

**Additional context**
Full changelog:
https://github.com/ComposeGears/Valkyrie/blob/main/tools/idea-plugin/CHANGELOG.md


**Expected behavior**

```md
**Added**

- 1
```

title: Support extracting release notes file from changelog
author: Goooler
body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**

Now we have to extract the release notes string and write it to shell like

https://github.com/JetBrains/gradle-changelog-plugin/blob/c3902fd5754ba2622dd101be3c97a05359142e83/.github/workflows/build.yml#L126

It would be better to support writing it to file directly, as we can pass the file to `gh` cli like 

```sh
# Use release notes from a file
gh release create v1.2.3 -F release-notes.md
```
See https://github.com/cli/cli/blob/19633d38554c0dfce870d1a63703af514941d878/pkg/cmd/release/create/create.go#L122-L123.

This feature may help avoid the char escaping in issues in shell like https://github.com/ffurrer2/extract-release-notes/issues/349.


**Additional context**

Something related to https://github.com/ffurrer2/extract-release-notes.

title: Access additional data when building section URL
author: HarrisonMc555
body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
The default implementation of the `sectionUrlBuilder` is an implementation of `ChangelogSectionUrlBuilder` that builds the appropriate URL to view the commits from one release to another in GitHub. However, it unsurprisingly does not work for other Git servers. I wrote an implementation of `ChangelogSectionUrlBuilder` that constructs a URL to view commits from one release to another in Bitbucket. However, there is one issue. If I am constructing a URL to look at unreleased changes, I cannot simply have an open-ended ".." like you can in a GitHub URL.

At first, I thought I needed to explicitly include the default branch (e.g. `master`, `main`, etc.) as the endpoint for the range of commits. So, my first implementation retrieved a property from `gradle.properties` and used it when constructing the URL. It looked something like this:

```kotlin
changelog {
    repositoryUrl.set(properties("repositoryUrl").get())
    // ...
    sectionUrlBuilder.set(ChangelogSectionUrlBuilder { repositoryUrl, currentVersion, previousVersion, isUnreleased ->
        // The default implementation creates a GitHub URL, this code creates a Bitbucket URL.
        val prefix = versionPrefix.get()
        val parameters = mutableListOf<String>()
        if (previousVersion != null) parameters.add("since=refs%2Ftags%2F$prefix$previousVersion")
        val untilRef = if (isUnreleased || currentVersion == null) properties("repositoryDefaultBranch").get() else "refs%2Ftags%2F$prefix$currentVersion"
        parameters.add("until=$untilRef")
        parameters.add("merges=include")
        val parametersString = parameters.joinToString(separator = "&") { it }
        "$repositoryUrl?$parametersString"
    })
}
```

However, if I used this, I would get the following error:

```
FAILURE: Build failed with an exception.

* What went wrong:
Configuration cache problems found in this build.

1 problem was found storing the configuration cache.
- Task `:getChangelog` of type `org.jetbrains.changelog.tasks.GetChangelogTask`: cannot serialize Gradle script object references as these are not supported with the configuration cache.
  See https://docs.gradle.org/8.2/userguide/configuration_cache.html#config_cache:requirements:disallowed_types
```

From my understanding (and best guess), I believe this is because attempting to call the `properties` function requires the anonymous implementation of `ChangelogSectionUrlBuilder` to store a reference to the Gradle script object.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
The `ChangelogSectionUrlBuilder.build` method takes in all of the parameters that most users would need. However, users of Bitbucket or other Git servers may need additional information. I think it would be reasonable to add an "extra parameters" value (or something of the sort) that users could optionally use when constructing the URL. If needed, they could fill the "extra parameters" value much like they fill the `repositoryUrl` or `versionPrefix` field. They could access them inside the `sectionUrlBuilder` in the same way that they access `versionPrefix`.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
I tried using the `extra` and `extraProperties` fields. The `extra` field reported the same "cannot serialize Gradle script object references" issue. The `extraProperties` field had the error "Cannot get property 'repositoryDefaultBranch' on extra properties extension as it does not exist".

**Additional context**
As it turns out, I was able to get the Bitbucket URL to work without needing the default branch. However, it does seem like this could be a necessary change for other Git servers or even just users with other requirements, so I decided to create this issue anyways.

title: Changelog moved from Unreleased to Released section is malformed
author: hrach
body:

It seems, that the automation creating PRs for the new release malforms output when moving content from the unreleased section to the released section. It happened to me two times:

- https://github.com/nextras/orm-intellij/pull/104/files

It seems to be related to backticks.

**Environment:**

title: `Execution failed for task ':getChangelog'. > Collection is empty.`
author: bric3
body:
**Describe the bug**
It seems that `getChangelog` task fails on the very first changelog generation with no previous released items. 

```
org.gradle.api.tasks.TaskExecutionException: Execution failed for task ':getChangelog'.
	at org.gradle.api.internal.tasks.execution.ExecuteActionsTaskExecuter.lambda$executeIfValid$1(ExecuteActionsTaskExecuter.java:130)
	at org.gradle.internal.Try$Failure.ifSuccessfulOrElse(Try.java:293)
	...
Caused by: java.util.NoSuchElementException: Collection is empty.
	at kotlin.collections.CollectionsKt___CollectionsKt.first(_Collections.kt:201)
	at org.jetbrains.changelog.tasks.GetChangelogTask.run(GetChangelogTask.kt:110)
```

The stack trace points to this piece of code

```kotlin
      when {
                version != null -> get(version)
                unreleased -> unreleasedItem ?: throw MissingVersionException(unreleasedTerm.get())
                else -> releasedItems.first() // <--------
            }
```

**To Reproduce**
Have an changelog without released items

`CHANGELOG.md`
```markdown
<!-- Keep a Changelog guide -> https://keepachangelog.com -->

# Rectangle Actions Plugin Changelog

## [Unreleased]

### Changed

- Bootstrap plugin
```

`build.gradle.kts`
```kotlin
plugins {
  id("org.jetbrains.changelog") version("2.2.1")
}

// Configure Gradle Changelog Plugin - read more: https://github.com/JetBrains/gradle-changelog-plugin
changelog {
  path = "${rootProject.projectDir}/CHANGELOG.md"
  repositoryUrl = providers.gradleProperty("pluginRepositoryUrl")
  itemPrefix = "-"
  version = providers.gradleProperty("pluginVersion")
  keepUnreleasedSection = true
  unreleasedTerm = "[Unreleased]"
  header = provider { "[${version.get()}] - ${date()}" }
  groups = emptyList()
}
```

Then run task `./gradlew getChangelog`

**Expected behavior**
The changelog, or a clearer error message. Maybe point to some task to emit unreleased section.

**Environment:**
 - OS: 15.0.1
 - Gradle Changelog Plugin Version 2.2.1
 - Gradle Version 8.10.2

**Additional context**
Add any other context about the problem here.

title: Patching reverses order of links when not using a semantic versioning
author: novotnyr
body:
**Describe the bug**
When not using a SemVer, the list of links at the end of the Changelog is patched to a reverse order.

**To Reproduce**

1. Create a changelog with a non-semantic versioning. 

```
# Changelog

## Unreleased

### Added

### Changed

### Fixed

## 1.2 - 2024-01-08

### Added

- No major stuff in here, but still

## 1.1 - 2024-01-08

### Added

- Version 1.1 stuff

## 1.0 - 2024-01-08

### Added

- Some stuff

[Unreleased]: https://github.com/novotnyr/gradle-changelog-poc/compare/v1.2...HEAD
[1.2]: https://github.com/novotnyr/gradle-changelog-poc/compare/v1.1...v1.2
[1.1]: https://github.com/novotnyr/gradle-changelog-poc/compare/v1.0...v1.1
[1.0]: https://github.com/novotnyr/gradle-changelog-poc/commits/1.0
```
2. Configure `headerParserRegex = Regex("""(\d+\.\d+)""")` to indicate a nonsemver versioning.
3. Run `./gradlew patchChangelog`
4. The order of links will be reversed:

```
[Unreleased]: https://github.com/novotnyr/gradle-changelog-poc/compare/v1.2...HEAD
[1.0]: https://github.com/novotnyr/gradle-changelog-poc/commits/1.0
[1.1]: https://github.com/novotnyr/gradle-changelog-poc/compare/v1.0...v1.1
[1.2]: https://github.com/novotnyr/gradle-changelog-poc/compare/v1.1...v1.2
```

**Expected behavior**
The order of the links should not be patched to an last-to-recent version order. It should remain ordered as it is (most recent version on top, the past versions in decreasing version order). This is in line with [Keep a Changelog](https://keepachangelog.com/en/1.1.0/) semantics.

**Environment:**
 - OS: MacOS
 - Gradle Changelog Plugin Version 2.2.0
 - Gradle Version:  8.6

title: Unable to run changelog method in script plugins
author: KorewaLidesu
body:
**Describe the bug**
Unable to run changelog method in script plugins.
If the code below put in `build.gradle` then it will work, but if won't on script plugin.

I can't reload with `--stacktrace` arguments. So here is [the other option](https://gist.github.com/KorewaLidesu/570e3b81da2bacf88870eb5326511ddf) I have 

Code:
```
private String parseChangelog() {
    if (propertyBool('publish_with_changelog')) {
        if (!file('CHANGELOG.md').exists()) {
            throw new GradleException('publish_with_changelog is true, but CHANGELOG.md does not exist in the workspace!')
        }
        String parsedChangelog =
            changelog.renderItem(
                    changelog.getLatest().withHeader(false).withEmptySections(false),
                    Changelog.OutputType.MARKDOWN)
        if (parsedChangelog.isEmpty()) {
            throw new GradleException('publish_with_changelog is true, but the changelog for the latest version is empty!')
        }
        return parsedChangelog
    }
    return null
}
```

**To Reproduce**
Reload project.

**Expected behavior**
Changelog should be printed.

**Environment:**
 - OS: Window
 - Gradle Changelog Plugin Version: 2.2.0
 - Gradle Version: 8.3

**Additional context**
No infos.

title: patchChangeLog breaks CHANGELOG.md file
author: hauner
body:
**Describe the bug**

`patchChangelog`  breaks my `CHANGELOG.md`. This used to work with the 1.x versions.

**To Reproduce**

checkout https://github.com/openapi-processor/openapi-processor-intellij and run `patchChangelog`.

Below is the result. The latest version looks ok, but it deletes most of the older versions and the remaining versions do not look right.

I'm using a `headerParserRegex`, not sure if this has any influence.

It still goes wrong if I add a `.0` patch version to all versions that do not have a patch version.

```
<!-- Keep a Changelog guide -> https://keepachangelog.com -->

# openapi-processor-intellij Changelog

## [Unreleased]

## [2023.3-SNAPSHOT.1] - 2023-12-03

- support mapping format v5 (since openapi-processor-spring/micronaut 2023.6)

## [[2023].1-SNAPSHOT.1]

- support mapping format v3 (since openapi-processor-spring/micronaut 2023.1)

## [[2022].1-SNAPSHOT.1]

- 2022.1 compatibility

## [[2021].1-SNAPSHOT.1]

- auto-detect the `mapping.yaml` file and set the json schema for editing support (autocompletion & validation)
- add openapi-processor icon for `mapping.yaml`

[Unreleased]: https://github.com/openapi-processor/openapi-processor-intellij/compare/v2023.3-SNAPSHOT.1...HEAD
[2021]: https://github.com/openapi-processor/openapi-processor-intellij/commits/v2021
[2022]: https://github.com/openapi-processor/openapi-processor-intellij/compare/v2021...v2022
[2023]: https://github.com/openapi-processor/openapi-processor-intellij/compare/v2022...v2023
[2023.3-SNAPSHOT.1]: https://github.com/openapi-processor/openapi-processor-intellij/compare/v2023...v2023.3-SNAPSHOT.1
```

The original file looks like this (I stripped some older versions): 

```
<!-- Keep a Changelog guide -> https://keepachangelog.com -->

# openapi-processor-intellij Changelog

## [Unreleased]
- support mapping format v5 (since openapi-processor-spring/micronaut 2023.6)

## 2023.2
- support mapping format v4 (since openapi-processor-spring/micronaut 2023.3)

## 2023.2-SNAPSHOT.1
- support mapping format v4 (since openapi-processor-spring/micronaut 2023.3)

## [2023.1]
- support mapping format v3 (since openapi-processor-spring/micronaut 2023.1)

## [2023.1-SNAPSHOT.1]
- support mapping format v3 (since openapi-processor-spring/micronaut 2023.1)

## [2022.4]
- Idea 2022.3 compatibility
- allow future Idea builds
- increased minimum version to Idea 2020.3

## [2022.3]
- support mapping format v2.1 (since openapi-processor-spring/micronaut 2022.5)

## [2022.3-SNAPSHOT.1]
- support mapping format v2.1 (since openapi-processor-spring/micronaut 2022.5)

## [2022.2.1]
- Idea 2022.2 compatibility
```


**Expected behavior**

.. it should properly update the changelog.

**Environment:**
 - Gradle Changelog Plugin Version [2.2.0]
 - Gradle Version [8.4]

title: Generated markdown is invalid according to markdownlint
author: brodock
body:
**Describe the bug**
Running the `patchChangelog`, removes the empty line between the Level 2 and Level 3 Headers and any list item present there, which is considered invalid by markdownlint.

**To Reproduce**

1. Include items in the unreleased section.
2. Run `patchChangelog` task
3. Run `markdownlint` against `CHANGELOG.md`

This is an example of the violations:

```
CHANGELOG.md:15 MD022/blanks-around-headings/blanks-around-headers Headings should be surrounded by blank lines [Expected: 1; Actual: 0; Below] [Context: "### Changed"]
CHANGELOG.md:16 MD032/blanks-around-lists Lists should be surrounded by blank lines [Context: "- Some content you added to the Unreleased section"]
```

**Expected behavior**
The generated Markdown should include an empty line after the Level 3 headers. This is enough to comply with both violations.

**Environment:**
 - OS: `macOS Ventura 13.5`
 - Gradle Changelog Plugin Version: `2.1.2`
 - Gradle Version: `8.2.1`

**Additional context**
Add any other context about the problem here.

title: Getting Changelog for a version fails when running through gradle wrapper
author: anikrajc
body:
**Describe the bug**
When running the following command,
```
 ./gradlew getChangelog -q --no-header --no-summary --version=1.0.1
```
The command fails with Gradle Wrapper help menu
```
Command-line option '--version' does not take an argument.

USAGE: gradlew [option...] [task...]
```

**To Reproduce**
Using 2.1.2 version of the library on a kotlin multiplatform project. You can reproduce this with any gradle project and running the command through the terminal.

**Expected behavior**
The changelog of the given version should have been displayed

**Environment:**
 - OS: macOS
 - Gradle Changelog Plugin Version 2.1.2
 - Gradle Version 7.6.1

title: Wrong render in changelog file that use CRLF
author: danygold
body:
**Describe the bug**
Plugin currently fully supports only `CHANGELOG.md` files that use `LF` line separator. Using a different line separator can leads wrong markdown/html/plain text render.
PR #177 resolve a regression introduced in 2.1.0 version of plugin, and take stability to the `patchchangelog` task, but there are some other bugs that are present.

Main bug are:
- `HTML render` generate wrong link
- `HTML render` generate an HTML that contains unnedeed new line
- `Plain text render` generate a wrong formatting (E.g. duplicated empty line)
- `Markdown render` add an unnecessary newline between links

**To Reproduce**
Please check [this test repository](https://github.com/danygold/gradle-changelog-plugin/tree/test/crlf_wrong_render). I added some unit test, and the result can be view at this [link](https://github.com/danygold/gradle-changelog-plugin/actions/runs/5499027970/jobs/10020984906)

**Expected behavior**
Output of the plugin is consistent with all line separator.

**Environment:**
 - OS: Windows
 - Gradle Changelog Plugin Version 2.1.1
 - Gradle Version 8.1.1

**Additional context**
This issue isn't a regression of a recent version of plugin (2.0.0 is the last version that i tested for a possible regression)

title: Properties `itemPrefix` and `lineSeparator` of class `org.jetbrains.changelog.Changelog.Item` are unused
author: rybak
body:
**Describe the bug**
Class `org.jetbrains.changelog.Changelog.Item`  has two unused properties: `itemPrefix` and `lineSeparator`. They are only used by private function `copy` to pass the value to the constructor.

**To Reproduce**
1. Add the following code to `src/test/kotlin/org/jetbrains/changelog/ChangelogTesting.kt` in the repository:
```kotlin
package org.jetbrains.changelog

fun main() {
    val changelog = Changelog(
        "", null, null, null, ChangelogPluginConstants.UNRELEASED_TERM, ChangelogPluginConstants.GROUPS,
        ChangelogPluginConstants.SEM_VER_REGEX,
        "+ This prefix from Changelog is used: ",
        null, { _, _, _, _ -> "" },
        "\n\n" // <---- this line separator is used
    )

    println(
        changelog.renderItem(
            Changelog.Item(
                "", "", "", false, mapOf("Example section" to setOf("Example", "of", "list")),
                " this itemPrefix is ignored", " the line separator from Item is ignored too"
            ),
            Changelog.OutputType.MARKDOWN
        )
    )
}
```
2. Launch function `main()`

**Actual behavior**
Output of the program uses the values of itemPrefix and lineSeparator from class `Changelog`:
```
## 



### Example section

+ This prefix from Changelog is used:  Example

+ This prefix from Changelog is used:  of

+ This prefix from Changelog is used:  list


```
though user of class `Item` might expect the values passed into the constructor to be used.

**Expected behavior**
Class `Changelog.Item` doesn't have the unused properties. Even though I'm experimenting with using the class `Item` directly, I'm OK with just dropping these properties, which would lead to a compilation error on upgrade, if the class is used directly.

Starting to use the properties doesn't seem useful. It might break changelog formatting unexpectedly after an upgrade, unlike a compilation error if they were just removed.

Maybe a deprecation period could be used, with eventual removal.

**Additional context**
Even though function `renderItem()` calls `with(item)`, properties `itemPrefix` and `lineSeparator` are `private` in class `Item`, so properties with the same name of the class `Changelog` get used in lines:

```kotlin
                yield(lineSeparator)
// [...]
                            .map { "$itemPrefix $it" }
```

title: org.jetbrains.changelog.Changelog.Item.plus doesn't keep `withHeader` and other boolean flags
author: rybak
body:
**Describe the bug**
I'm trying to _add_ additional list items to `<change-notes>` in the Gradle task `patchPluginXml` of Gradle IntelliJ Plugin. I'm trying to modify output of the Changelog Plugin. Unfortunately, function `org.jetbrains.changelog.Changelog.Item.plus` loses the value of `withHeader` and other boolean flags, which were set up in the call chain before `plus()` is invoked.

Below is a minimal example that doesn't require Gradle IntelliJ Plugin.

**To Reproduce**
This is code, slightly modified from [IntelliJ Platform Plugin Template](https://github.com/JetBrains/intellij-platform-plugin-template/blob/main/build.gradle.kts#L72-L102). Two methods `withHeader` and `withEmptySections` are used as an example â€“ these are just the two boolean flags that I've checked, others are probably affected in the same way. 

1. Put the following code at the top level of the `build.gradle.kts`, for simplicity:
```kotlin
with(project.changelog) {
	val extraItems = mapOf(
		"Extra section" to setOf("[Extra link](https://example.org)")
	)
	println(renderItem(
		getUnreleased()
			.withHeader(false)
			.withEmptySections(true)
			.plus(Changelog.Item("", "", "", false, extraItems, "", "")),
		Changelog.OutputType.MARKDOWN
	))
}
```
2. Put the following Markdown text in `CHANGELOG.md`:
```
# Changelog

## [Unreleased]

### Added
- Example added item, "Changed" is empty

### Changed

## [0.2.0-alpha]

[Unreleased]: https://github.com/rybak/intellij-copy-commit-reference/compare/v0.2.0-alpha...HEAD
[0.2.0-alpha]: https://github.com/rybak/intellij-copy-commit-reference/compare/v0.1.0-alpha.1...v0.2.0-alpha
```
3. Run `./gradlew`

**Actual behavior**
Header `## [Unreleased]` is printed in the log, empty section is absent:
```
## [Unreleased]

### Added
- Example added item, "Changed" is empty

### Extra section
- [Extra link](https://example.org)

[Unreleased]: https://github.com/rybak/intellij-copy-commit-reference/compare/v0.2.0-alpha...HEAD
```

**Expected behavior**
Header is _not_ printed in the log, empty section is present:
```
### Added
- Example added item, "Changed" is empty

### Changed

### Extra section
- [Extra link](https://example.org)

[Unreleased]: https://github.com/rybak/intellij-copy-commit-reference/compare/v0.2.0-alpha...HEAD
```

**Environment:**
 - Gradle Changelog Plugin Version 2.1.0
 - Gradle Version 8.1.1

**Additional context**
* Workaround â€“ call `with*` functions _after_ the `plus` function:
```kotlin
with(project.changelog) {
	val extraItems = mapOf(
		"Extra section" to setOf("[Extra link](https://example.org)")
	)
	println(renderItem(
		getUnreleased()
			.plus(Changelog.Item("", "", "", false, extraItems, "", ""))
			.withHeader(false)
			.withEmptySections(true),
		Changelog.OutputType.MARKDOWN
	))
}
```
* I'm not yet familiar with how `copy()` works in Kotlin, but this bug has something to do with the fact that [function `plus()`](https://github.com/JetBrains/gradle-changelog-plugin/blob/main/src/main/kotlin/org/jetbrains/changelog/Changelog.kt#L318-L327) is not calling [`private fun copy`](https://github.com/JetBrains/gradle-changelog-plugin/blob/main/src/main/kotlin/org/jetbrains/changelog/Changelog.kt#L289-L327). Instead, the [constructor, which sets the default values for affected flags](https://github.com/JetBrains/gradle-changelog-plugin/blob/main/src/main/kotlin/org/jetbrains/changelog/Changelog.kt#L254-L268) is invoked.

title: patchChangelog breaks changelog format in version 2.1.0
author: danygold
body:
**Describe the bug**
`patchChangelog` in version 2.1.0 breaks changelog format adding unneccessary new line.

**To Reproduce**
Use [CHANGELOG.md](https://github.com/JetBrains/gradle-changelog-plugin/files/11830060/CHANGELOG.md) and run `gradle patchChangelog`

**Expected behavior**
Changelog is patched and format is not manipulated.
- Version 2.0.0 - [CHANGELOG_2_0_0.md](https://github.com/JetBrains/gradle-changelog-plugin/files/11830085/CHANGELOG_2_0_0.md) (Expected behaviour)
- Version 2.1.0 [CHANGELOG_2_1_0.md](https://github.com/JetBrains/gradle-changelog-plugin/files/11830094/CHANGELOG_2_1_0.md) (Wrong changelog format)


**Environment:**
 - OS: Windows 11
 - Gradle Changelog Plugin Version 2.1.0
 - Gradle Version 8.1.1

**Additional context**
In previous version before 2.1.0, `patchCahngelog` works propertly as expected

title: Duplicated and incorrect repository links
author: krasa
body:
**Describe the bug**
- duplicated repository links after every `publishPlugin` https://github.com/krasa/StringManipulation/blob/test/CHANGELOG.md?plain=1
- incorrect link  https://plugins.jetbrains.com/plugin/2162-string-manipulation/versions/test/347843
![image](https://github.com/JetBrains/gradle-changelog-plugin/assets/1160875/85ff14ad-fe88-4130-a20b-31ed0b457fb8)


**To Reproduce**
- have an unreleased change `foo` https://github.com/krasa/StringManipulation/commit/2c1129a5f91874681ebad700c33425e54c6ea459
- increment plugin version and do `publishPlugin` -> result https://github.com/krasa/StringManipulation/commit/7a46157ceb32dd96d2409b0824dbf25c1b637e48 + https://plugins.jetbrains.com/plugin/2162-string-manipulation/versions/test/347843

**Expected behavior**
- not duplicating links
- proper link in the Whatâ€™s New section

**Environment:**
 - OS: Win10
 - Gradle Changelog Plugin Version 2.1.0
 - Gradle Version 8.1
 


**Additional context**
- disabled `org.gradle.configuration-cache` for testing purposes
- [changelog config ](https://github.com/krasa/StringManipulation/blob/7a46157ceb32dd96d2409b0824dbf25c1b637e48/build.gradle.kts#L80-L83)  + https://github.com/krasa/StringManipulation/blob/7a46157ceb32dd96d2409b0824dbf25c1b637e48/build.gradle.kts#L123-L132
